chapter.title: Overview
chapter.simpara: The QALIPSIS GUI allows you to create, configure, run, monitor and view results of test campaigns from your browser. You can configure campaigns and run them immediately, at a future time, or on a recurring schedule.

listitem.simpara: You can view in-process campaign results in real time.
You can view finalized reports of a previous campaign’s data on static reports and on an interactive time-series chart.

chapter.simpara: The following topics are discussed in this section:

listitem.simpara: Navigate to the QALIPSIS Welcome page (https://alpha.qalipsis.io)
Enter your Username and Password.
Select the tenant to use from the drop down box.
Tenant#1 allows access to the Campaign and Series pages.
Tenant#2 allows access to the Admin pages.
Click Submit to log into QALIPSIS.
If a Tenant selection message is not displayed, you will be logged in to QALIPSIS with the tenant you were using in your most recent login.
You can can select a different tenant at any time that you are logged into QALIPSIS.

chapter.title: Navigating QALIPSIS
chapter.simpara: When you log into QALIPSIS, the Home page of the GUI and a left sidebar is displayed.

section.title: Left Sidebar

listitem.simpara: Near the top of the sidebar, a menu is displayed. The contents of the menu depends on the tenant you are currently using and your profile permissions.
From the displayed menu you several options.
Near the bottom of the sidebar, your user name are displayed with your current tenant. Click on the your name to display the Tenant Modal window.

section.simpara: From the Tenant Modal you have a few options.

listitem.simpara: The left sidebar remains displayed throughout your session.
Click on your name near the bottom of the sidebar to open the Tenant Modal window.
Select an available tenant to use for the current session.
With Tenant #1 selected, the Campaign and Series screens are accessible by clicking on the appropriate link near the top of the sidebar.
With Tenant #2 selected, the Admin area of the interface is accessible (if you have Admin permissions).
Click on your name at the bottom of the left sidebar to open the Tenant Modal window.
Click on the settings icon.
Click on My profile to open the My Profile page.
Make changes to your profile and click on Save changes.
Click on your name at the bottom of the left sidebar to open the Tenant Modal window.
Click on the settings icon.
Click on My profile to open the My Profile page.
Click on Change password.
Enter your new password in the New Password and Comfirm Password fields.
Click om Save changes to save your new password.
Click on your name at the bottom of the left sidebar to open the Tenant Modal window.
Click on the settings icon.
Click the Logout button. You will be logged out and returned to the Welcome screen.

section.title: Access campaign information and functions
section.simpara: The Campaign page is accessible from the Tenant #1 left sidebar. From the Campaign page, you can view current campaign data, update a campaign, and create a new campaign.
Further details are provided later in this documentation.

section.title: Access Series information and functions
section.simpara: The Series screen is accessible from the Tenant #1 left sidebar. The Series' screen allows the user view current series information, update or duplicate a series, and create a new series.
Further details are provided later in this documentation.

section.title: Access administrative functions
section.simpara: From the Tenant2 sidebar (if you have Admin permissions):

listitem.simpara: Click on Admin to display the Users screen.
Click on a user’s name to open the Update User window.

section.simpara: From the Update User` window, user information can be updated and modified and roles (permissions) set.
More options will be available from the Admin` area of the GUI in the near future.

chapter.title: Working with series data and filters
chapter.simpara: Series are defined events and meters that are logged while a campaign is in progress.

listitem.simpara: The logged events and meters can be graphically displayed
You can create a series if your profile has write permissions.
You can update a series if you are the owner and/or have write permissions.
Click on Series to open the Series page.
The Series page displays a table listing currently defined series.
Click the +New series button near the top right corner of the page.
The New series modal window opens.
Enter the required data into the appropriate fields.
Click the Create button.
The following text is displayed in the modal window: '[Series Name] has just been created!'.
Close the New series modal window to return to the Series page.
The newly created series is displayed in the table.
Login as Tenant #1.
Click on Series in the left sidebar menu.
The Series page opens and displays a table listing currently defined series.
Click on the name of the series you want to update.
The Update series modal window opens.
Enter the required data into the appropriate fields.
Click the Update button.
Close the Update series modal window to return to the Series page.
Click the +Campaign button near the top right of the Campaign page.
The New Campaign window will open displaying the campaign name, a settings icon, a search field, and a table listing scenarios that are available to run in the campaign.
Optionally, rename the campaign by hovering over the New Campaign title and selecting the pen icon to edit the name.
Select the checkbox to the left of each scenario that you want to include in your campaign.
Each scenario has a default configuration.
To configure a scenario to better suit your specific test, click the Configure button to the right of the scenario.
Update the fields on the Scenario Configuration window.
Click Apply and close the window.
Scenario changes are saved for the current campaign and do not affect the scenario’s default settings.
Choose when you want to run your campaign.
Click the Run immediately button to start the campaign immediately with the the default run-time configuration.
Campaign data is displayed in real-time in the report window and on the time-series chart.
Minion count is displayed by default.
Select one or more series cards to display the generated data for that series.
Click the arrow next to the Run immediately button to configure custom runtimes.
The custom running modal window opens.
Select the date you want to start the campaign using the date picker tool.
The date format is [Month dd, yyyy] **Select the time zone for the campaign start time from the ‘Time zone’ dropdown.
The time zone format is [(UTC+hh:mm) Continent/ City].
If you want the campaign to run more than once, select a frequency from the [Frequency] drop down.
The frequency options are Daily, Weekly, and Monthly.
Daily runs the campaign at the previously selected time each day.
Weekly runs the campaign on the selected day(s) every week at the previously selected time of day.
Monthly runs the campaign each selected day(s) of every month at the previously selected time.

chapter.title: Displaying campaign data

listitem.simpara: Login to QALIPSIS as Tenant #1.
Click on Campaign in the left sidebar.
A table listing all existing and planned campaigns is displayed.
Click on any column title to sort the table by ascending/descending order.
Search for a specific campaign by clicking on the magnifying glass icon near the top right of the screen and entering the name (or partial name) of the campaign.
Click on the name of a campaign to display the Campaign details page.
A report box is displayed at the top of the screen.
The top line in the report box displays information for the whole campaign; the following lines display information for each scenario within the campaign.
Click the View more tooltip on the right side of the report box to expand the report box to display all scenarios in the campaign.
Click the More details tooltip to display the info for the total campaign in a dropdown box.
Click on the Campiagn Summary dropdown filter to select the scenario information to display in the report box.
Below the report box, a row of filter cards is displayed with the individual events and meters that are logged for the campaign.
Refer to Step Specifications or for guidance creating filtering steps.
Each card includes the Name, Type, and display color.
Below the row of filter cards, a time-series chart is displayed with the x-axis displaying the elapsed time and the y-axis displaying series data.

chapter.simpara: Select one or more series cards to display the generated data for that series.
